plan: 1. Create abandonment detail types — Add `AbandonmentDetail` interface to `src/domain/types.ts` with fields: `id` (string enum-like: 'outdated-copyright', 'stale-news-date', 'broken-badge', 'dead-counter', 'deprecated-browser-notice', 'missing-asset-placeholder', 'expired-ssl-notice', 'defunct-partner-logo'), `label` (display text), and `cssClass` (for styling). Add `abandonmentDetails: readonly AbandonmentDetail[]` to the `GeneratedSite` interface in `src/domain/generate-site.ts`.

2. Create abandonment fragments dataset — New file `src/domain/data/abandonment-fragments.ts` containing era-keyed arrays of abandonment detail templates. Examples per era: early-web → ['Under Construction — check back soon!', 'This page requires Shockwave Flash', 'Visitor counter: {count}'], portal-era → ['Powered by IE6', 'Last updated: {staleDate}', 'Yahoo! GeoCities badge'], web2 → ['Digg this!', 'RSS Feed (0 subscribers)', 'Adobe Flash required'], startup-minimalism → ['Beta invite — join {count} others on the waitlist', 'Launching Q{quarter} {staleYear}']. Include broken badge URLs, dead counter values, deprecated browser notices, and missing asset placeholder text. Export from `src/domain/data/index.ts`.

3. Create abandonment detail generator — New file `src/domain/generators/abandonment-generator.ts` following the metadata-generator pattern. Function signature: `generateAbandonmentDetails(seed, era, archetype, region): AbandonmentDetail[]`. Uses `deriveSeed(seed, 'abandonment')` for deterministic RNG. Picks 2–5 details from era-compatible fragments, fills template placeholders ({count}, {staleDate}, {staleYear}) with deterministic values derived from yearFounded. Export from `src/domain/generators/index.ts`.

4. Integrate generator into site generation pipeline — In `src/domain/generate-site.ts`, call `generateAbandonmentDetails()` after metadata generation and add result to the returned `GeneratedSite` object.

5. Create abandonment rendering function — New file `src/render/render-abandonment-details.ts` that takes `GeneratedSite` and renders each `AbandonmentDetail` as DOM elements injected into appropriate locations. Detail types map to render strategies: 'broken-badge' → greyed-out image placeholder div with alt text, 'dead-counter' → visitor counter widget with frozen number, 'deprecated-browser-notice' → banner at top of page, 'missing-asset-placeholder' → broken image icon with '[Image not found]' text, 'stale-news-date' → handled by news-block module, 'outdated-copyright' → handled by footer override, 'expired-ssl-notice' → small warning bar, 'defunct-partner-logo' → faded placeholder boxes.

6. Integrate abandonment details into rendering pipeline — In `src/render/render-homepage.ts`, call abandonment rendering after module rendering. Some details inject into existing sections (footer copyright year override, news date staleness), others create new decorative elements (broken badge strip, counter widget, browser notice banner). Pass abandonment details to `renderFooter()` to optionally override copyright year to look outdated.

7. Add CSS for abandonment detail elements — Add styles to `src/styles/generated-site.css` for: `.abandonment-badge` (greyed-out, desaturated filter), `.abandonment-counter` (retro visitor counter styling), `.abandonment-browser-notice` (yellow/amber warning banner), `.abandonment-missing-asset` (broken image icon + dashed border placeholder), `.abandonment-partner-logos` (faded, low-opacity boxes). All styles must use existing CSS custom properties for theme consistency.

8. Write test file `tests/us-012-abandonment-details.test.ts` — Following existing test patterns (lazy imports, snapshot tests, determinism checks). Test cases: (a) file structure verification, (b) generator returns 2–5 details for all era/archetype combos, (c) determinism — same seed produces identical details across 50 runs, (d) era compatibility — details are appropriate to the selected era, (e) no DOM dependencies in generator, (f) snapshot stability across archetypes/eras/seeds, (g) detail labels are non-empty strings, (h) main content remains readable (DOM test — verify .site-body text content is not obscured by abandonment elements).
9. Run lint, typecheck, and tests — Execute `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to verify all acceptance criteria pass and no regressions are introduced.
testing_recommendations: Determinism: Run generator with same seed 50+ times, assert identical output each time
Count bounds: Verify every era/archetype/region combination produces exactly 2–5 abandonment details
Era appropriateness: Confirm early-web sites never get startup-era details and vice versa
Content readability: DOM test asserting .site-body main content text is present and not hidden by abandonment overlays
Snapshot stability: 24+ snapshots across archetypes × seeds × eras to catch unintended output drift
No real network failures: Verify no actual fetch/XHR calls are made — all broken effects are CSS/DOM only
RNG isolation: Confirm abandonment generator uses deriveSeed('abandonment') and doesn't affect other generators' output
risks: Adding abandonmentDetails to GeneratedSite changes the interface used by all renderers — existing snapshot tests for US-008 and US-011 will need updating
Template placeholder substitution ({count}, {staleDate}) must handle all edge cases to avoid visible template syntax leaking into rendered output
Abandonment CSS must not break layout on mobile — broken badge placeholders and counter widgets need responsive sizing
The 2–5 count requirement with era filtering means some eras need at least 5 compatible fragments to avoid running out of options
Injecting abandonment elements into existing sections (footer, news) couples the abandonment system to those modules — changes to footer/news rendering could break abandonment display
